records.ety: [F. doctrine, L. doctrina, fr. doctor. See Doctor.]
usage: -- Doctrine, Precept. Doctrine denotes whatever is recommended as a speculative truth to the belief of others. Precept is a rule down to be obeyed. Doctrine supposes a teacher; precept supposes a superior, with a right to command. The doctrines of the Bible; the precepts of our holy religion.

quotes: He taught them many things by parables, and said unto them in his doctrine, Hearken.

defs: That which is taught; what is held, put forth as true, and supported by a teacher, a school, or a sect; a principle or position, or the body of principles, in any branch of knowledge; any tenet or dogma; a principle of faith; as, the doctrine of atoms; the doctrine of chances.
quotes: Unpracticed he to fawn or seek for power By doctrines fashioned to the varying hour.
